professionalSummary: Approximately 5.5 years designing and building production engineering systems: concurrent Go backends for DNS intelligence and durable data processing, plus a company-wide Playwright QA platform that translates human-readable test contracts into deterministic CI execution and evidence.
experience: Architecting concurrent Go services with worker pools, bounded processing, lifecycle management, and graceful shutdown
Designing durable DNS data ingestion, resolution, indexing, and query workflows
Building REST and Unix-socket interfaces for backend systems
Creating a schema-driven test generation platform from DSL contracts to Playwright TypeScript
Integrating test management, CI execution, scheduling, reporting, traces, and video evidence
Building reproducible Docker environments and GitLab CI/CD workflows
Designing monitoring, profiling, validation, and automated test strategies
Writing architecture, operator, troubleshooting, and authoring documentation
Mentoring interns and supporting maintainable engineering practices
principles: Systems should remain understandable years later
questions: How does identity persist through change?
How do simple rules create unexpected outcomes?
